name: Company Intro
summary: Standard company-overview deck: who you are, what's distinctive about you, who you serve, and why they win. Built for first-touch sales meetings, partner intros, and 'about us' decks attached to outbound emails.

beats.name: Logo
beats.description: Cover slide with the logo, tagline, and a single line of positioning. Set the visual tone — confident, branded, uncluttered.

beats.name: About
beats.description: Capture the company in three or four lines: what you do, who you do it for, and how long you've been at it. Avoid jargon; an outsider should grok the business in fifteen seconds.

beats.name: Competitive Advantage
beats.description: State the one or two things you do that the alternatives can't. Frame it as a structural advantage, not a feature list. The audience should leave able to repeat the differentiator in their own words.

beats.name: Company Story
beats.description: Share the founding insight or origin moment — why this company exists, told as a short story. Specific people, specific problems, a specific 'aha.' Story beats biography.

beats.name: Offerings
beats.description: Map the products and services into a coherent picture. Show how they fit together rather than listing them flat. The shape of the portfolio is itself a message.

beats.name: Ideal Customer
beats.description: Define who you serve best. Industry, size, role, situation. Specificity here helps the audience self-select and signals you understand the customer well enough to be choosy.

beats.name: Key Benefits
beats.description: Translate offerings into three to five customer outcomes. Lead with the result, not the mechanism. Each benefit should answer 'what becomes easier or better for me?'

beats.name: Success Story
beats.description: Tell one named customer's story with concrete before/after metrics. A single specific story is more persuasive than a logo grid; pick one that mirrors the audience you're presenting to.

beats.name: Closing & CTA
beats.description: Close with one line of why now and one specific next step — a discovery call, a pilot, a partner program. End with contact info that's easy to act on without searching.
